middleware.description: Thin wrapper over entitlement.enforce that specifically checks the `facilities` (basic) or `facilities_advanced` (Enterprise) entitlement per route. On refusal, returns 402 `FACILITY_FEATURE_NOT_ENTITLED` with an upgrade CTA in the response envelope's `.errors[0].meta.upgrade_url`. Reads the entitlement key from the middleware parameter (e.g. `facility.enforce_entitlement:facilities_advanced`).

Reads facility_id from the request body (create/update flows) or the X-Facility-Id header (rare — booking widgets pass via body). Loads the Facility model, checks tenant scoping (refuses when facility.tenant_id != request tenant), and stores it on RequestContext::facility() so downstream services can call `Request::activeFacility()` without re-fetching. Publishes the `activeFacility` + `activeFacilityId` macros on the Request class. Optional (skipped when the request carries no facility_id — most read routes).

consumed.note: The permission-based authorize middleware wraps the ability checks defined in policies.json + permissions.json. Route-level `permission` metadata resolves through this middleware.

ResolveScope narrows queries by the caller's active branch/organization scope. Applied globally to authenticated tenant routes; facility routes rely on it for the branch narrowing described in policies.json.
